title: Progress tracking library with CLI wrapper
body:
## Context

Build a progress tracking library for Node.js that formats completion status in multiple styles and exposes a CLI wrapper. All modules must be CommonJS with no external dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `formatProgress(complete, total)` returns correct human-readable strings.
- [ ] Overflow values are clamped to 100%.
- [ ] CLI wrapper accepts two integer arguments and prints formatted progress to stdout.
- [ ] `npm test` and `npm run build` pass at every stage.
- [ ] No external dependencies — only Node.js built-ins.

## Notes

These are integration-level criteria; individual task criteria are on child issues.

title: Implement formatProgress core formatter
complexity_rationale: Single-file implementation with straightforward arithmetic and string formatting; no design decisions or edge cases beyond basic math.
body:
## Context

Add the foundational `formatProgress(complete, total)` function that returns a human-readable string like `"2/5 complete (40%)"`. This is the base module that all other features build on.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/progress.js` exports a `formatProgress(complete, total)` function via CommonJS.
- [ ] `formatProgress(2, 5)` returns `"2/5 complete (40%)"`.
- [ ] Percentage is computed as `Math.round(complete / total * 100)`.
- [ ] Uses only built-in Node.js modules.
- [ ] `test/progress.test.js` covers at least: zero progress, partial progress, full completion.
- [ ] `npm test` passes.

## Notes

No external dependencies allowed. CommonJS only (no ESM).

title: Add overflow clamping for progress values exceeding total
complexity_rationale: Small addition to a single file — adds a Math.min clamp guard and a few extra test cases. No new modules or APIs.
body:
## Context

Progress values where `complete > total` must be clamped so they never exceed 100%. This hardens the formatter against invalid inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `formatProgress(7, 5)` returns `"5/5 complete (100%)"` (both the displayed count and percentage are clamped).
- [ ] A `percent(complete, total)` helper returns `100` when `complete > total`.
- [ ] Existing tests for normal-range inputs still pass.
- [ ] New tests in `test/progress.test.js` cover: `complete == total`, `complete > total`.
- [ ] `npm test` passes.

## Notes

Depends on core-formatter being in place first.

title: Add CLI wrapper that prints formatted progress to stdout
complexity_rationale: New CLI entry point with argument parsing, error handling, integration with core module, and end-to-end test coverage.
body:
## Context

Expose the formatter through a CLI script so users can call it from the command line with two integer arguments.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/cli.js` is executable and parses two integer positional arguments from `process.argv`.
- [ ] Running `node src/cli.js 3 10` prints `"3/10 complete (30%)"` to stdout.
- [ ] Overflow clamping is respected (e.g., `node src/cli.js 7 5` prints `"5/5 complete (100%)"`).
- [ ] Tests cover the CLI success path.
- [ ] `npm test` and `npm run build` pass.

## Notes

Depends on overflow-clamping being done first. Must use CommonJS and only Node.js built-ins.
